title: AgentDocument
description: The v2 AGTP Agent Document. Canonical identity record served at DESCRIBE on Form 1 and Form 1a URIs. Mirrors core.identity.AgentDocument. v1 documents are converted at load time by from_dict_v1_compat and emerge as in-memory v2 with document_version='v1-migrated'.

agtp_version.description: AGTP wire-protocol version this document is valid for. Today: '1.0'.

document_version.description: Schema version of the document itself. v2 is current. 'v1-migrated' identifies a v1 document auto-converted at load.

agent_id.description: Canonical Agent-ID. SHA-256 hex of the agent's Genesis document per draft-hood-independent-agtp-08.

name.description: Human-readable agent name. Not unique across servers; the agent_id is.

principal.description: Display name of the human or entity the agent acts on behalf of.

principal_id.description: Stable identifier of the principal (email, opaque id, organization handle).

description.description: One- or two-sentence description of the agent.

status.description: Operational lifecycle state. 'active' agents accept traffic; 'suspended' agents return 503; 'retired' agents return 410; 'deprecated' agents still accept traffic but signal planned retirement (clients SHOULD migrate). Added 'deprecated' v1.3.0.

skills.description: Prose declarations of what this agent can do. The primary 'what does this agent do' surface. Replaces v1's 'capabilities' field.

requires.description: Structured declaration of methods, scopes, and wildcard policy the agent expects to handle inbound.

methods.description: AGTP verbs this agent is authorized to handle inbound. Methods absent here return 403 method-not-permitted-for-agent unless wildcards is true.

scopes.description: Authority scopes the agent expects to be presented for invocation.

wildcards.description: When true, the agent accepts any method (orchestrator pattern). Subject to per-server policies.wildcards_accepted.

scopes_accepted.description: Authority scopes this agent will honor on inbound requests.

issued_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the document was issued.

issuer.description: Domain or identifier of the issuing authority.

trust_tier.description: Trust tier per draft-hood-independent-agtp §6.2. 1 = Verified (DNS / log / hybrid-anchored), 2 = Org-Asserted (default), 3 = Experimental. Defaults to 2 when omitted. (Added v1.2.0.)

verification_path.description: Verification path that backs the trust_tier claim. 'dns-anchored' / 'log-anchored' / 'hybrid' are Tier 1 production paths per AGTP-TRUST; 'org-asserted' is the Tier 2 production path (organization-signed, no external proof); 'self-signed' is a code-only Tier 2 marker for dev / local / test deployments. (Added v1.2.0; 'org-asserted' added v1.3.0.)

trust_warning.description: Per §6.2, Tier 2 Agent Documents MUST carry a trust_warning. The daemon auto-populates 'verification-incomplete' for Tier 2 documents that don't set it; clients display it as a trust indicator. Omitted (or empty) when not applicable. (Added v1.2.0.)

owner_id.description: Legal owner — the entity that registered this agent. Mirrors the Agent Genesis's owner_id when one is loaded. Surfaced on responses as the Owner-ID header. Empty when no Genesis backs this agent. (Added v1.2.0.)

role.description: Identity role per draft-hood-agtp-merchant-identity-02. 'agent' (default) follows the standard dispatch path; 'merchant' triggers mod_merchant's PURCHASE counterparty gate. Mirrors the Agent Genesis's role field. (Added v1.3.0.)

manifest_issuer.description: Identifier of the registrar that signed this AgentDocument (Tier 3.2). Empty when the manifest is unsigned (operator-only assertion). (Added v1.3.0.)

manifest_issuer_public_key.description: PEM-encoded Ed25519 public key the verifier uses to validate manifest_signature. Empty when the manifest is unsigned. (Added v1.3.0.)

manifest_signature.description: base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature over the canonical-JSON form of this document with manifest_signature set to empty string. Empty when the manifest is unsigned. Daemons MUST verify on load and refuse to host agents whose signature does not validate. (Added v1.3.0.)
